title: RSA migration: audit remaining OPENSSL_EXTRA users and measure code size
description:
After RSA cfg gates are cleaned up, audit ALL remaining wolfssl_openssl_extra usages across the workspace and determine whether OPENSSL_EXTRA can be removed from wolfssl-src/user_settings.h.

Audit steps:
1. grep -r 'wolfssl_openssl_extra' --include='*.rs' to list all remaining users
2. For each user: determine if it's RSA (should be gone), or something else still legitimately needing it
3. Check wolfcrypt-ring-compat: does it use any EVP/OPENSSL_EXTRA functions?
4. Check wolfcrypt-dpe and wolfcrypt-dpe-hw: do they use any OpenSSL compat functions?
5. If all non-RSA users have been migrated or confirmed unnecessary, remove #define OPENSSL_EXTRA from wolfssl-src/user_settings.h

Code size measurement:
- Build wolfssl-src with and without OPENSSL_EXTRA
- Record .text size delta via nm/size on libwolfssl.a
- Document expected saving in commit message

Acceptance criteria:
- All remaining wolfssl_openssl_extra users are documented with rationale (keep vs migrate)
- If OPENSSL_EXTRA is removed: full test suite still passes; code size saving is documented
- If OPENSSL_EXTRA cannot yet be removed: a follow-up issue is filed for each remaining blocker
